info.name: NME - Business Browse API (v2)
info.description:
Covers the 5 endpoints behind category grid / subcategory list / business listing / search / business detail screens: List/Search Businesses, Get Service, Get Sub Service, Get Sub Service V1, Get Business. All public — no auth required.

Known gaps as of 2026-07-16, confirmed against live production responses:
- List/Search Businesses does NOT return rating, avg_rating, is_verified, or distance — and has no Latitude/Longitude param at all.
- Get Sub Service V1 accepts Latitude/Longitude but they currently have no effect on the response (same output with or without them) — not actually wired to distance/sorting yet.
- image is the field name (not image_url) on List/Search Businesses and Get Service/Sub Service, already a full URL not a filename. On Get Business, note the *object itself* has a raw `image` filename plus a computed `logo_url` (full URL) — inconsistent with the other endpoints, which only expose the full-URL form.
- Pagination uses total/page/total_pages — there is no has_more field.
- Get Business's `photos` array does NOT include ratings/reviews — use the separate Get Reviews endpoint (business-reviews-api collection) for that.

A reusable Haversine distance-calculation SQL pattern already exists elsewhere in the codebase (getOfferV1) and smart_update (the business table) does have latitude/longitude columns, so distance support is buildable into List/Search Businesses if needed — just not wired in yet.

2026-07-16: Get Business (and add_business_photos/delete_business_photo) were fixed after being found broken on production — a table-name case-sensitivity bug (extraimage vs the correct extraImage) crashed on Linux/production MySQL while working fine on local Windows/MySQL. Confirm the fix is deployed before relying on this endpoint.

query.description: Optional. Filter by main category id (from Get Service).
Optional. Filter by subcategory id (from Get Sub Service).

request.description:
Public — no auth needed. Paginated business listing/search — all filters optional and combinable. Not a port of v1's GetBusinessAll/homeSearch: those do a per-business photo subquery (N+1) that times out unfiltered at full scale (19k+ businesses). This one returns only the main image (full photo gallery is on Get Business detail), so it stays fast at any scale — verified at 902 businesses (category_id=30, "Shopping") in ~0.35-0.75s. Only status=1 (active) businesses are returned.

Does NOT currently return: rating/avg_rating, is_verified, distance. No Latitude/Longitude param exists yet.

response.name: Success
response.body:

  "status": 1,
  "message": "Businesses fetched successfully",
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 20,
  "total": 19864,

    {
      "id": "1",
      "businessName": "Example Tailors",

      "location": "Example City",
      "mobileNumber": "0000000000",
      "whatsappNumber": "0000000000",
      "serviceId": "30",
      "sub_service_id": "7303",
      "image": "https://api.nmeapp.in/public/upload/master/example.jpg",
      "status": "1",
      "dt_created": "2026-06-02 22:26:28",
      "service_name": "Shopping",
      "subServiceName": "Groom Store"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Public — no auth needed. All main business categories, including a live business_count per category. Use the returned id as category_id / cat_id in List/Search Businesses and Get Sub Service.

formdata.description: Required. Main category id, from Get Service.

request.description: Public — no auth needed. Subcategories for one main category, including a live business_count per subcategory, plus a top-level urls array of category banner images.

Public — no auth needed. Same as Get Sub Service, accepts Latitude/Longitude — but verified against production: passing them does NOT currently change the response (no distance field added, no reordering). Effectively identical output to Get Sub Service today.

response.name: Success (same shape as Get Sub Service, no distance field)

request.description:
Public — no auth needed. Single business by id, with its full photo gallery. Every smart_update column is included at the top level of data (not just a curated subset) plus two computed fields: logo_url (full URL, derived from the raw image filename) and photos (array). Note image itself remains a raw filename here, unlike the other Business Browse endpoints where image is already a full URL — check logo_url instead if you need a ready-to-use URL for the main image.

Ratings are NOT included — call Get Reviews (business-reviews-api collection) separately for average_rating/total_reviews/reviews.
